decision: WAVE05-SEC-001 architecture review: conditional sign-off with one follow-up risk
reason: Runtime/bootstrap owns JWT signing-key loading, proxy trust, CORS, and HSTS/header posture for the touched serving path; buildRouter fails closed without an injected JWT key; no new security platform tree was introduced. Targeted boundary checks and Go package tests passed with local cache paths.
impact: Follow-up: backend/security/ratelimit.go still has a legacy TRUST_PROXY_HEADERS env fallback in getClientIP, though server wiring uses injected proxy trust via RateLimitConfig.

decision: WAVE05-SEC-005 is cross-cutting but should stay a stop-and-review/docs inventory slice; do not broaden into deferred security-platform work.
reason: Touched state spans runtime security config, auth seams, server middleware behavior, OpenAPI/API follow-up docs, and active production notes. The remaining actionable seam is narrow: migrate handlers/markets/resolvemarket.go from direct JWT parsing/raw http.Error responses to injected AuthService plus shared authhttp/ReasonResponse handling.
impact: Use architecture-agent/openapi-contract-agent/test-reliability-agent for review as needed, then require software-action-verifier-agent signoff focused on remaining boundary-security inventory, active-note accuracy, and explicit deferral of MFA/RBAC/distributed rate limiting/request signing/session redesign/compliance.
